Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Delta Suspends Flights On Major Long-Haul Route (Full Schedule Inside)

Delta's latest development follows other airlines in the market. See the latest information here!

Delta's route suspension signals ongoing capacity constraints in long-haul markets as carriers reassess profitability amid elevated operating costs.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 25, 2026

New Images Show B-2 Spirit Bombers Departing for Iran Mission with Unknown White Patches on Wings

Recently released photos show B-2 stealth bombers preparing to launch for a mission over Iran last week, with multiple unidentified white patches added along the leading edges of their wings. The U.S. Central Command has released new photos of the B-2A Spirit stealth bomber, one of the assets that,...

B-2 Spirit bombers deployed to Iran showed unexplained white patches on wing leading edges, suggesting potential modifications for enhanced stealth or sensor capabilities not previously disclosed.

See The Aviationist →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

Digital Airman Certificate Bill Clears U.S. House

Measure would permit pilots to present electronic credentials during FAA inspections.

Digital airman certificates could reduce administrative friction and fraud while enabling real-time credential verification through FAA systems.

See AVweb →

14 CFR Part 5 provides a standardized framework for systematically evaluating safety hazards rather than relying on intuition or past experience alone.

Military Mar 25, 2026

Brazil unveils first Gripen E fighter assembled on home soil

Brazil has unveiled the first Gripen E fighter assembled on its own soil, a milestone for the Saab-Embraer…

Brazil's domestic Gripen E assembly establishes local aerospace manufacturing sovereignty and positions Embraer to compete in regional defense supply chains.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

China Eastern orders over 100 Airbus aircraft, plans to retire at least 53 A320s

China Eastern Airlines has placed a $15.802 billion Airbus order for 101 A320neo family aircraft, comprised of A320neo,…

China Eastern's massive A320neo order signals confidence in narrow-body demand recovery while simultaneously accelerating fleet modernization through older A320 retirements.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 25, 2026

UK, Turkey sign Eurofighter Typhoon support deal to advance fighter export

The UK and Turkey have signed a new support and training agreement tied to Ankara’s Eurofighter Typhoon purchase,…

Strategic support partnerships on existing fighter contracts can unlock additional training and sustainment revenues while strengthening geopolitical ties with key NATO allies.

See AeroTime Hub →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

The SMS Declaration of Compliance formalizes operators' commitment to safety management systems and creates measurable accountability benchmarks that the FAA will use to assess organizational maturity beyond traditional compliance audits.

Wizz Air COO on recovering the cost lead, offering Wi-Fi, and hedging fuel

On March 25, 2026, Wizz Air announced that it had signed a deal with Barcelona-based inflight technology provider…

Low-cost carriers are now bundling ancillary services like premium Wi-Fi to defend market share against competitors while maintaining their cost advantage.

See AeroTime Hub →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

House Moves to Bring Back Civil Overland Supersonic Flight

Measure would direct FAA to write new regulations for civil aircraft flying faster than Mach 1.

Regulatory reform could unlock a commercial supersonic market worth billions if operators can navigate noise mitigation requirements over populated areas.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Make-Or-Break Week: United Airlines Enters Final Contract Talks With Flight Attendants

Representatives of the carrier's cabin crew are hoping to be able to lock down a tentative agreement.

United's final contract week signals potential resolution to labor pressures that have strained crew resources and operational reliability across the industry.

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit design flaws at major hubs like LAX create systematic incursion risks that require operational procedure updates beyond pilot awareness alone.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

DJI just nuked drone prices across the board

Something strange is happening to DJI drone prices right now. And if you’ve ever even briefly considered buying a drone, this is one of those moments where ignoring it might come back to haunt you. Because this isn’t a typical seasonal sale or a small round of discounts tucked away on a product...

DJI's across-the-board price cuts signal potential market consolidation or inventory clearance, creating a rare window for consumers but potentially masking competitive pressure in the commercial drone sector.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Air Arabia debuts Panasonic Avionics multi-orbit IFC

Already a customer of Panasonic Avionics’ eXW wireless inflight entertainment system, UAE-based Air Arabia is now flying with the IFEC giant’s Ku-band multi-orbit onboard connectivity solution, Runway Girl Network can reveal. The all-Airbus A320 Family operator is among a cluster of carriers...

Air Arabia's adoption of Panasonic's multi-orbit Ku-band connectivity expands IFEC provider options for low-cost carriers seeking reliable global coverage without single-satellite dependency.

See Runway Girl Network →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

FAA Letter Says Whiteman Airport Obligated to Stay Open

Los Angeles County still continues to study closure.

Despite county closure studies, FAA regulatory obligations may legally bind Whiteman Airport open, creating a potential conflict between local policy and federal compliance requirements.

See AVweb →

▶
Safety Mar 25, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

Reno-Tahoe's Mustang 9 Departure requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural complexity on runways 17L/R that could impact departure safety.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Philippine carriers cut flights, secure fuel as government declares emergency

The Philippines has become the first country to declare a national state of energy emergency amid escalating Middle…

Airlines facing forced capacity reductions due to government fuel rationing signals how geopolitical energy crises can rapidly constrain carrier operations beyond demand management.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Southwest Airlines Cabin Reconfiguration Increases Seat Pitch, Reducing Overall Passenger Capacity

Southwest Airlines has added extra-legroom seats to its fleet.

Southwest's shift toward premium seating reflects cabin reconfiguration prioritizing passenger comfort and ancillary revenue over maximizing seat count, a strategic departure from its traditional high-volume model.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

$15.8 Billion Vote Of Confidence: Airbus Scores Massive 101-Plane Deal

The European planemaker continues to make big steps forward in a key market.

Airbus's 101-aircraft order signals sustained commercial demand recovery and validates its competitive positioning against Boeing during a critical market reconsolidation period.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 25, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-airspace systems offers a more immediate path to sustainability than relying solely on aircraft technology transitions.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Why Can’t The Airbus A350 Be Powered By Any Other Engine Type?

The Airbus A350 and Rolls-Royce Trent XWB are a match made in aviation heaven.

The A350's fuselage design and aerodynamic architecture were engineered specifically around the Trent XWB's dimensions and performance, making retrofit with alternative engines structurally and thermally infeasible.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

JetBlue Flight Halted After Bizarre Runway Encounter With Coyote

The aircraft did not suffer any damage and was back in the air shortly after the incident, transporting passengers to their original destination.

Wildlife incursions at major airports represent an ongoing operational vulnerability that can disrupt schedules despite minimal physical damage, highlighting the need for enhanced perimeter management protocols.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

Warehouse drones are quietly transforming alcohol distribution across America

In a move that shows just how fast warehouse automation is evolving, Corvus Robotics has teamed up with Southern Glazer’s Wine & Spirits to scale autonomous inventory drones across one of the largest beverage distribution networks in the US. more…

Autonomous warehouse drones are enabling major beverage distributors to reduce labor costs and accelerate order fulfillment in logistics operations previously dependent on manual inventory management.

See DroneDJ →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

Bay Area next as Wing expands drone delivery network

The Wing team has announced plans to expand its ultra-fast residential drone delivery service across the San Francisco Bay Area in the coming months, marking a full-circle moment for the company that was originally born inside Google X back in 2012. more…

Wing's Bay Area expansion signals growing regulatory acceptance of residential drone delivery as viable last-mile logistics infrastructure beyond initial markets.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

14+ Hours Nonstop: Delta Air Lines Reveals Brand New Long-Haul Route

Delta Air Lines has disclosed another new long-haul route: Los Angeles to Manila. While it has not been officially announced yet, it was revealed in the airline's response to Philippine Airlines' and its application to the US Department of Transportation to begin flights from Manila to Chicago...

Delta's undisclosed LA-Manila route signals strategic defensive positioning against Philippine Airlines' competitive US expansion plans.

See Simple Flying →

Air Cargo Mar 25, 2026

Meet The New King Of Cargo: Atlas Air Orders 20 Airbus A350Fs

Well, it looks like in a few years’ time, the United States will have ANOTHER A350-operator alongside Delta… but it won’t be flying passengers! That’s because Airbus announced on March 16th that Atlas Air Worldwide has placed a firm order for 20 Airbus A350F freighters, making it the largest...

Atlas Air's A350F order signals a major shift in cargo operations toward next-generation wide-body freighters, potentially reshaping competitive dynamics in premium freight markets.

See Simple Flying →

Porter to launch 12 routes out of new Montreal Metropolitan Airport terminal

Porter Airlines will be the launch carrier at Montreal Metropolitan Airport (YHU) brand new terminal. Starting on June…

Porter's expansion into Montreal's new airport signals a strategic shift toward secondary hubs that can compete with legacy carriers while leveraging modern infrastructure.

Hundreds of TSA agents resign as new DHS head says reopening is ‘No. 1 priority’

Hundreds of Transportation Security Administration (TSA) officers have resigned during a partial government shutdown that first started in…

TSA workforce attrition during shutdowns directly threatens aviation security operations and could delay passenger screening across commercial airports nationwide.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Air France Has Ended Airbus A380 Flights On 18 Routes: Full List

Uncover the hidden gems among the Air France's A380 routes, including the five airports that only saw the carrier's superjumbos.

Air France's A380 withdrawal from 18 routes signals accelerating retirement of four-engine aircraft as airlines prioritize fuel-efficient twin-engine widebodies for post-pandemic network optimization.

Military Mar 25, 2026

Photos of A-10s Used to Strike Iranian Naval Vessels Show Video Game-Inspired Nicknames

Images shared by U.S. Central Command suggest that at least some A-10s taking part in Operation Epic Fury carry new video game-inspired nicknames, an operationally irrelevant detail but one likely to interest spotters and modelers. As we already reported a few days ago, the U.S. Air Force A-10...

A-10 Warthogs conducting anti-naval operations in the Middle East are displaying video game-inspired nose art, reflecting evolving aircrew culture and morale practices in modern combat operations.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 25, 2026

Lockheed Martin to quadruple Precision Strike Missile production

Lockheed Martin said it has signed a new framework agreement with the US Department of War to accelerate…

Quadrupling PSM production signals sustained U.S. military modernization demand, creating aerospace supply chain pressures and contract opportunities for defense contractors.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Singapore Airlines to launch daily flights to new Western Sydney Airport

Singapore Airlines announced that it will begin daily nonstop service between Singapore’s Changi Airport (SIN) and the newly…

Singapore Airlines' commitment to daily service signals strong confidence in Western Sydney Airport's viability and positions the carrier to capture growing Asia-Pacific travel demand from Australia's fastest-developing region.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

What Are The Benefits Of Flying Emirates Business Class?

The benefits extend beyond the cabin and begin well before boarding.

Emirates' business class value proposition encompasses ground services and pre-flight experiences that competitors must match to remain competitive in premium segment pricing.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Press Release: Immfly, Wizz Air, and gateretail partner on digital cabin

Immfly, Wizz Air, and gateretail have launched an integrated digital cabin ecosystem that unifies onboard retail, passenger and crew connectivity, and operational data into a single platform, marking a significant step forward in the digital transformation of Europe’s commercial aviation sector....

Unified digital ecosystems consolidating retail, connectivity, and operations reduce fragmentation costs and enhance crew efficiency across airline fleets.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

United Airlines eases long-haul travel for families with new economy beds

Long-haul flights for families may become a little easier in the future after United Airlines unveiled plans to…

United's economy bed product targets the high-margin family travel segment by addressing sleep comfort on transcontinental routes, potentially increasing ancillary revenue and customer loyalty.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 25, 2026

DOT&E Says No Combat-Capable TR-3-Configured F-35s Were Delivered in FY2025

The latest Director, Operational Test & Evaluation report says no combat-capable F-35 TR-3 aircraft have been delivered to the U.S. military until September 2025 because of the truncated software. The recently released 2025 Director, Operational Test & Evaluation (DOT&E) annual report brought...

F-35 TR-3 deliveries remain stalled due to incomplete software, signaling persistent integration challenges affecting military modernization timelines.

See The Aviationist →
